Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amir Sarreshtehdary of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Mark Mulliner, as Personal Representative of the Estate of David Roberts, deceased; and Beverly Roberts and Davi Breslin, as Wrongful Death Heirs of David Roberts, deceased
Mr. Mark Mulliner and witness Ms. Jennifer Mulliner appeared in person; and Ms. Beverly Roberts and Ms. Davi Breslin appeared via CourtCall.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER TO APPORTION AND DISTRIBUTE FUNDS

Hearing held. Plaintiffs Mark Mulliner, Beverly Roberts, Davi Breslin and witness Jennifer Mulliner were sworn individually. The Court examined each of the plaintiffs and the witness, details on record. After the examination the Court ruled on plaintiffs' motion for order to apportion and distribute funds in the following proportions:

Mark Mulliner, as Personal Representative of the Estate of David Roberts, deceased 50%
Beverly Roberts, as Wrongful Death Heir 25%
Davi Breslin, as Wrongful Death Heir 25%
Total 100%

The Court further ordered that the signature of plaintiff Mark Mulliner alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Off Calendar Feb-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsay Weiss of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (213-622-7300), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties - David Roberts / Mark Mulliner, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. On the issue of duty, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention that defendant owed decedent a duty. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating the duty element of plaintiffs' claims. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Dr. Cohen's declaration would create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to decedent. As to the borrowed servant issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that its employees were special employees of the refinery/refineries at issue such that plaintiffs' exclusive remedy lies in the workers' compensation scheme. The Court did not consider defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication due to defendant's violation of the requirements set forth in CRC 3.1350(b), (d) and plaintiffs' objections thereto. Defendant's request for a continuance was denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
